sources.description: Yandex Disk REST API landing page.
Yandex Forms API overview.
Telemost API reference index.
Yandex Tracker API overview.
Yandex 360 API overview.
Yandex 360 service application examples for Mail IMAP and Calendar CalDAV with OAuth tokens.
Yandex Mail OAuth authorization documentation for IMAP and SMTP.
Yandex contacts/CardDAV client setup documentation with carddav.yandex.ru endpoint.
Yandex Calendar sync setup documentation naming CalDAV server and account URL.
Yandex 360 Connector documentation stating that it syncs Calendar, tasks, and contacts over CalDAV and CardDAV.
WebDAV HTTP extensions used underneath CalDAV and CardDAV.
CalDAV protocol method and report definitions.
CardDAV protocol method and report definitions.
IMAP4rev1 command definitions.
SMTP AUTH extension.
SMTP protocol command definitions.
SASL OAuth mechanisms used by IMAP/SMTP XOAUTH2-style authentication.
Current yandex-office code and markdown files.
Yandex 360 API access page listing Directory OAuth permissions.
Yandex Forms API access page listing forms:read and forms:write permission semantics.

CalDAV principal object is returned.
methods.notes: Yandex official docs name CalDAV service configuration, but no official Yandex page found publishes a PROPFIND command example. This row is protocol-spec and live-probe coverage.

methods.success_evidence: Calendar list is returned.
methods.notes: Yandex official docs name CalDAV service configuration, but no official Yandex page found publishes a PROPFIND calendar-home command example. This row is protocol-spec and live-probe coverage.

methods.success_evidence: CalDAV event list is returned.
methods.notes: Yandex official docs do not publish a REPORT date-search command example. This row is protocol-spec and live-probe coverage.

request.data: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?><C:calendar-query xmlns:D="DAV:" xmlns:C="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:caldav"><D:prop><D:getetag/><C:calendar-data/></D:prop><C:filter><C:comp-filter name="VCALENDAR"><C:comp-filter name="VEVENT"><C:time-range start="{calendar_start}" end="{calendar_end}"/></C:comp-filter></C:comp-filter></C:filter></C:calendar-query>

methods.success_evidence: HTTP 201 or 204.
methods.notes: Directly covered by Yandex service-application docs: explicit CalDAV PUT examples create single and recurring events at /calendars/<user_email>/events-default/<event_uid>.ics; the same text says PUT creates or changes an event.

methods.success_evidence: HTTP 200 iCalendar object.
methods.notes: Directly covered by Yandex service-application docs: explicit curl examples read the event .ics resource after creation.

methods.success_evidence: HTTP 204.
methods.notes: Yandex service-application docs show calendar deletion through the Python caldav library, but no official Yandex page found publishes an explicit event-resource DELETE example. This row is protocol-spec and live-probe coverage.

methods.success_evidence: CardDAV principal/addressbook home is returned.
methods.notes: Yandex official docs name CardDAV service configuration, but no official Yandex page found publishes a PROPFIND command example. This row is protocol-spec and live-probe coverage.

methods.success_evidence: CardDAV PROPFIND multistatus response.
methods.notes: Yandex official docs name CardDAV service configuration, but no official Yandex page found publishes a PROPFIND addressbook listing command example. This row is protocol-spec and live-probe coverage.

Yandex official docs describe CardDAV sync and creating contacts through a CardDAV account, but no official Yandex page found publishes a vCard PUT command example. This row is protocol-spec and live-probe coverage.

methods.success_evidence: HTTP 200 vCard.
methods.notes: Yandex official docs describe CardDAV sync, but no official Yandex page found publishes a vCard GET command example. This row is protocol-spec and live-probe coverage.

methods.success_evidence: HTTP 204.
methods.notes: Yandex official docs describe CardDAV sync, but no official Yandex page found publishes a vCard DELETE command example. This row is protocol-spec and live-probe coverage.
